Package global.thalion.ttio.analysis

Class TwoDCos

java.lang.Object
global.thalion.ttio.analysis.TwoDCos
public final class TwoDCos extends Object
2D-COS compute primitives — Noda's synchronous / asynchronous decomposition from a perturbation series (Hilbert-transform approach).

Given a dynamic-spectra matrix A of shape (m, n) (m perturbation points × n spectral variables) and a reference spectrum (default: column-wise mean), the dynamic matrix Ã = A − reference is decomposed into:

  • Synchronous Φ = (1/(m−1)) · Ãᵀ · Ã, symmetric.
  • Asynchronous Ψ = (1/(m−1)) · Ãᵀ · N · Ã, antisymmetric, where N is the discrete Hilbert-Noda transform matrix.

All matrices are stored row-major as flat double[].

Cross-language equivalents: Python ttio.analysis.two_d_cos, Objective-C TTIOTwoDCos.

Since:
0.12.0

  • Method Summary

    Modifier and Type
    Method
    Description
    static TwoDimensionalCorrelationSpectrum
    compute(double[] dynamicSpectra, int m, int n)
    Compute the mean-centered 2D-COS decomposition.
    static TwoDimensionalCorrelationSpectrum
    compute(double[] dynamicSpectra, int m, int n, double[] reference, AxisDescriptor variableAxis, String perturbation, String perturbationUnit, String sourceModality)
    Compute the 2D-COS decomposition with full metadata and optional explicit reference spectrum.
    static double[]
    disrelationSpectrum(double[] synchronous, double[] asynchronous)
    Return |Φ| / (|Φ| + |Ψ|) — synchronous dominance in [0, 1].
    static double[]
    hilbertNodaMatrix(int m)
    Return the discrete Hilbert-Noda transform matrix of size (m, m), row-major.

    Methods inherited from class java.lang.Object

    clone, equals, finalize, getClass, hashCode, notify, notifyAll, toString, wait, wait, wait

  • Method Details

    • hilbertNodaMatrix

      public static double[] hilbertNodaMatrix(int m)
      Return the discrete Hilbert-Noda transform matrix of size (m, m), row-major. Entry (j, k) is 0 on the diagonal and 1 / (π · (k − j)) off-diagonal.
      Parameters:
      m - matrix order (must be ≥ 1)

    • compute

      public static TwoDimensionalCorrelationSpectrum compute(double[] dynamicSpectra, int m, int n)
      Compute the mean-centered 2D-COS decomposition.
      Parameters:
      dynamicSpectra - row-major flat double[] of length m * n
      m - number of perturbation points (rows)
      n - number of spectral variables (cols)

    • compute

      public static TwoDimensionalCorrelationSpectrum compute(double[] dynamicSpectra, int m, int n, double[] reference, AxisDescriptor variableAxis, String perturbation, String perturbationUnit, String sourceModality)
      Compute the 2D-COS decomposition with full metadata and optional explicit reference spectrum.
      Parameters:
      dynamicSpectra - row-major flat input, length m * n
      m - perturbation points
      n - spectral variables
      reference - length-n baseline to subtract (null = column mean)
      variableAxis - forwarded to the returned spectrum
      perturbation - forwarded to the returned spectrum
      perturbationUnit - forwarded to the returned spectrum
      sourceModality - forwarded to the returned spectrum

    • disrelationSpectrum

      public static double[] disrelationSpectrum(double[] synchronous, double[] asynchronous)
      Return |Φ| / (|Φ| + |Ψ|) — synchronous dominance in [0, 1]. Cells where both matrices vanish yield Double.NaN.
